title: Alberta Supports Scrapping the EV Mandate: Premier Smith
content: Prime Minister Mark Carney has scrapped the federal EV mandate, which required 60% of new cars sold in Canada to be zero-emission by 2030 and 100% by 2035. The mandate was criticized as unrealistic, particularly for Alberta, where only 19,863 EVs were registered in 2025, falling far short of the required 133,258 EVs in 2030. The policy was seen as damaging to Alberta's economy, raising costs, limiting consumer choice, and having minimal impact on global emissions. The provincial government supports consumer choice and believes incentives, rather than mandates, are key to promoting EV sales. They will monitor the impact of new emissions standards closely.
author: Analysts Account
